neighborhoods.name: Peninsula
neighborhoods.description: Iconic peninsula tip with the Casapueblo artist village, yacht harbor, and the famous Hand sculpture
neighborhoods.character: Peninsula resort, iconic landmarks, yacht harbor

housing.predominantType: Apartments and houses on the peninsula

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Punta del Este. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Punta del Este

neighborhoods.name: La Barra
neighborhoods.description: Bohemian-chic beach community across the undulating bridge with art galleries and surf culture

housing.predominantType: Beach houses, boutique properties, and apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Punta del Este's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Punta del Este

neighborhoods.name: Maldonado
neighborhoods.description: Adjacent city serving as the commercial center with year-round population and lower costs

housing.predominantType: Apartments and houses at lower resort prices

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Punta del Este

neighborhoods.name: Jose Ignacio
neighborhoods.description: Exclusive fishing-village-turned-luxury-destination with celebrities, estancias, and pristine beaches

housing.predominantType: Luxury beach houses, estancia properties, and boutique homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Punta del Este
